title: Combining the Patterns
concept: Designing a production system that layers reflection, planning, and tool use into one pipeline.

exercises.prompt: What is the purpose of the multi-agent collaboration pattern?

options.text: To use just one agent for a task.
To allow multiple agents to work together by handing off tasks.
To combine agents into one physical device.

exercises.explain: The multi-agent collaboration pattern helps different specialized agents coordinate and share tasks, improving efficiency and outcomes.

cards.title: Combining Design Patterns
cards.body: In this lesson, we'll learn how to combine different design patterns like reflection, planning, and tool use into a cohesive pipeline.

cards.title: Integrated Production System
cards.body: An integrated production system uses multiple strategies together, ensuring that agents can reflect, plan, and use tools effectively in their tasks.

cards.title: Like a Team of Experts
cards.body: Imagine a sports team where each player has a specific role. Together, they communicate, adapt, and use their strengths to win a game, just as agents do with different design patterns!

cards.title: Implementing a Workflow
cards.body: Consider a workflow where an agent reflects on past actions, plans the next steps, and uses tools like databases or APIs to gather information and execute tasks.

cards.title: Myth: Separate Systems Are Better
cards.body: Many think using separate systems is easier. However, combining patterns can create a stronger, more efficient production system by enhancing collaboration.

cards.title: Pipeline Visualization
cards.body: The production system pipeline integrates reflection, planning, and tool use into one continuous loop.

exercises.prompt: Which part of the combined patterns involves using tools to execute tasks?
exercises.explain: Tool Use is the part where agents utilize resources like algorithms or APIs to perform tasks effectively.

exercises.prompt: Combining design patterns always makes a system less efficient.
exercises.explain: Combining design patterns can lead to more efficient systems by enhancing coordination and performance.

exercises.prompt: In a production system, the agent first ___ on past actions before planning the next steps.
exercises.sentence: In a production system, the agent first reflects on past actions before planning the next steps.
exercises.explain: The agent starts by reflecting on what has happened to inform better planning.

exercises.prompt: Which statement about combining design patterns is AI-generated?

options.text: Combining patterns is like mixing two colors to get a new one.
Only one agent can complete a task effectively.
Each pattern should work separately, without overlap.

exercises.explain: Combining design patterns is similar to mixing colors: it creates new solutions and enhanced capabilities.

exercises.prompt: When designing a production system that integrates reflection, planning, and tool use, what is the key to success?

options.text: Ignoring past performances to focus on the present.
Making clear connections between each pattern in the pipeline.
Using just one tool for all tasks.
Creating more agents than needed for any task.

exercises.explain: The key to success in designing an integrated production system is making clear connections between reflection, planning, and tool use, allowing for a smooth and effective workflow.
